title: Q2 renewal forecast variance tightens to 1.7%
anchor: Actual renewal ARR landed at $1.77M versus the $1.8M forecast, marking the tightest variance in four quarters.
connect: Trailing four-quarter average variance runs at 8.2%.
body: Actual renewal ARR landed at $1.77M versus the $1.8M forecast, marking the tightest variance in four quarters. Trailing four-quarter average variance runs at 8.2%.
trace: Q2 2026 delivered 1.7% variance versus historical 8.2% average, signaling improved forecast discipline.

title: Mid-market NRR accelerates to 112% in Q2
anchor: Mid-market segment NRR sits at 112% for Q2 2026, pulling ahead of the trailing three-quarter average of 104.9%.
connect: Multi-product customers in this segment drive the expansion engine at 124% NRR.
body: Mid-market segment NRR sits at 112% for Q2 2026, pulling ahead of the trailing three-quarter average of 104.9%. Multi-product customers in this segment drive the expansion engine at 124% NRR.
trace: Mid-market NRR jumped 7 points above trailing average, with multi-product adoption as the primary expansion driver.

title: Multi-product customers expand at 124% NRR versus single-product 102%
anchor: Multi-product customers across 46 accounts delivered 124% NRR in Q2, while single-product customers across 74 accounts held at 102%.
connect: Multi-product share represents 38.3% of the customer base currently.
body: Multi-product customers across 46 accounts delivered 124% NRR in Q2, while single-product customers across 74 accounts held at 102%. Multi-product share represents 38.3% of the customer base currently.
trace: 22-point NRR spread between multi-product and single-product cohorts reveals the expansion engine mechanics.

title: At-risk ARR concentration drops to $220K across 3 accounts
anchor: April at-risk pool sits at $220K ARR across 3 accounts, down from March's $310K across 7 accounts.
connect: Top-20-ARR accounts in the at-risk pool decreased from 5 to 2 over the same period.
body: April at-risk pool sits at $220K ARR across 3 accounts, down from March's $310K across 7 accounts. Top-20-ARR accounts in the at-risk pool decreased from 5 to 2 over the same period.
trace: At-risk portfolio contracted by $90K and 4 accounts month-over-month, with improvement concentrated in top-ARR tier.

title: Mid-market time to first value compresses to 23 days
anchor: Q2 2026 mid-market new-customer cohort reached first value at 23 days across 24 accounts.
connect: Q1 2026 mid-market cohort required 38 days across 22 accounts.
body: Q2 2026 mid-market new-customer cohort reached first value at 23 days across 24 accounts. Q1 2026 mid-market cohort required 38 days across 22 accounts.
trace: 15-day TTFV improvement quarter-over-quarter in mid-market onboarding suggests process refinement.

title: Mid-market license utilization above 80% increases to 22 customers
anchor: Mid-market customers above 80% license utilization reached 22 in Q2 2026, up from 14 in Q1.
connect: Mid-market customers running Atlas Insights totals 24, with 18 expanding ARR during Q2.
body: Mid-market customers above 80% license utilization reached 22 in Q2 2026, up from 14 in Q1. Mid-market customers running Atlas Insights totals 24, with 18 expanding ARR during Q2.
trace: 8-customer increase in high-utilization mid-market accounts correlates with Atlas Insights product adoption.

title: Health score distribution shifts to 77% green in mid-market
anchor: Mid-market health distribution shows 77% green (34 of 44 accounts) in Q2 2026 versus 70% green (31 of 44) in Q1.
connect: Q1 green-health customers renewed at 97% while yellow-health customers renewed at 78%.
body: Mid-market health distribution shows 77% green (34 of 44 accounts) in Q2 2026 versus 70% green (31 of 44) in Q1. Q1 green-health customers renewed at 97% while yellow-health customers renewed at 78%.
trace: 7-point improvement in green-health distribution with Q1 green cohort demonstrating 97% renewal rate.

title: Customer health reviews generate 8 expansion opportunities worth $340K
anchor: April expansion opportunities sourced from customer health reviews total 8 opportunities representing $340K.
connect: March customer-health-review opportunities reached 6, with sales accepting 5 of them (83%).
body: April expansion opportunities sourced from customer health reviews total 8 opportunities representing $340K. March customer-health-review opportunities reached 6, with sales accepting 5 of them (83%).
trace: Health-review-sourced expansion opportunities increased by 2 opportunities month-over-month with strong sales acceptance.

title: Coverage tier retention spreads 14 points between high-touch and tech-touch
anchor: High-touch coverage delivered 96% GRR across 22 accounts in Q2 while tech-touch coverage achieved 82% GRR across 98 accounts.
connect: High-touch represents 18.3% of the customer base, concentrated in top-ARR accounts.
body: High-touch coverage delivered 96% GRR across 22 accounts in Q2 while tech-touch coverage achieved 82% GRR across 98 accounts. High-touch represents 18.3% of the customer base, concentrated in top-ARR accounts.
trace: 14-point GRR spread between coverage tiers with high-touch serving the top-ARR segment exclusively.

title: 90-day retention improves to 88% across new-customer cohorts
anchor: Q1 2026 all-segments new-customer cohort shows 88% retention at 90 days across 60 accounts with 26-day time to first value.
connect: Q4 2025 cohort achieved 83% retention across 55 accounts with 38-day time to first value.
body: Q1 2026 all-segments new-customer cohort shows 88% retention at 90 days across 60 accounts with 26-day time to first value. Q4 2025 cohort achieved 83% retention across 55 accounts with 38-day time to first value.
trace: 5-point improvement in 90-day retention correlates with 12-day TTFV improvement between cohorts.

title: Enterprise segment renewal performance holds at 94% GRR
anchor: Q2 2026 enterprise renewing book achieved 94% GRR at 106% NRR across $432K ARR.
connect: Enterprise NRR runs 6 points below mid-market's 112% over the same period.
body: Q2 2026 enterprise renewing book achieved 94% GRR at 106% NRR across $432K ARR. Enterprise NRR runs 6 points below mid-market's 112% over the same period.
trace: Enterprise segment demonstrates strong retention but lags mid-market expansion velocity by 6 NRR points.

title: Customer health reviews outperform outbound expansion sourcing
anchor: March customer-health-review expansion opportunities achieved 83% sales acceptance (5 of 6) versus outbound opportunities at 41% acceptance (7 of 17).
connect: April health review opportunities increased to 8 total worth $340K.
body: March customer-health-review expansion opportunities achieved 83% sales acceptance (5 of 6) versus outbound opportunities at 41% acceptance (7 of 17). April health review opportunities increased to 8 total worth $340K.
trace: Health review sourcing demonstrates 42-point higher sales acceptance rate than outbound expansion efforts.

title: TTFV under 30 days correlates with 91% renewal rates
anchor: Q1 2026 mid-market cohort customers reaching first value under 30 days renewed at 91% versus 74% for customers taking over 60 days.
connect: Q2 2026 mid-market new-customer cohort averaged 23-day time to first value across 24 accounts.
body: Q1 2026 mid-market cohort customers reaching first value under 30 days renewed at 91% versus 74% for customers taking over 60 days. Q2 2026 mid-market new-customer cohort averaged 23-day time to first value across 24 accounts.
trace: 17-point renewal rate advantage for sub-30-day TTFV with Q2 cohort positioning well at 23-day average.

title: Executive sponsor depth correlates with enterprise renewal signing

connect: Beacon Logistics signed their $280K renewal on April 21, ahead of the July 15 contract end date.
body: April 2026 enterprise renewals show 7 of 10 signed renewals had executive sponsors whose depth deepened during Q1 2026. Beacon Logistics signed their $280K renewal on April 21, ahead of the July 15 contract end date.
trace: 70% of enterprise renewals correlate with Q1 executive sponsor engagement deepening, with early signing patterns.

connect: Multi-product share of the customer base sits at 38.3% currently.
